subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Acquire and preprocess open-source models and evaluation datasets, complete the software and hardware environment configuration required for the proposed solution

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements:
Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, capable of using basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-drive
Role Count: 1 person

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Environment Configuration Required Resources
Input Introduction:

Check whether the hardware of the running environment meets the following requirements: GPU preferably NVIDIA A10 or above (optional), GPU memory >= 16GB (optional), CPU >= 8 cores, memory >= 16GB, operating system Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+).
Input Example:
Please list a checklist for self-inspection:
NVIDIA A10 or above (optional)
GPU memory >= 16GB (optional)
CPU >= 8 cores
Memory >= 16GB

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Environment Configuration Required Resources Ready
Output Introduction: Server has been configured with GPU driver (optional), Python environment, Node environment, meeting the hardware and system requirements for model deployment

subsections.title: Estimated Time
content: 1-2 days

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Install and configure Claude Code through Node

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Frontend Engineer/Backend/Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements: Familiar with Node
Role Count: 1

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Install and Configure Claude Code
Input Introduction: Install and configure Claude Code based on Node environment
Input Example:
Related commands are as follows:
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Configure environment variables (take ~/.bashrc as an example)
Configure models (~/.claude/settings.json)
Startup confirmation command, enter 'claude' to access command line, if you receive corresponding replies after entering any text without errors, the configuration is complete
Resource Links:
Claude Code GitHub: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Available Claude Code Service
Output Introduction: Automatically call relevant MCP services through Claude Code to complete enterprise-level introduction video production

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build ffmpeg-mcp service, supporting video compression, multi-track video burning, audio/video file extraction and other scenario video editing operations

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Backend/Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements: Familiar with Linux common commands, shell, ffmpeg and Python
Role Count: 1

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: ffmpeg-mcp Service Build and Startup
Input Introduction: Install and start relevant MCP services by installing related Python dependencies
Input Example:
System-level ffmpeg installation (taking Ubuntu as an example): sudo apt update && sudo apt install ffmpeg
requirements.txt contains: loguru>=0.7.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, mcp>=1.0.0, ffmpeg-python>=0.2.0

Python code implements FastMCP service, providing tools such as video duration retrieval, audio extraction, video stream extraction, video duration compression, audio duration retrieval, silence audio generation, multi-track video creation
Startup command: python server.py
Resource Links:
mcp-python-sdk GitHub: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk
ffmpeg Official: https://www.ffmpeg.org/
ffmpeg GitHub: https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg
ffmpeg-python GitHub: https://github.com/kkroening/ffmpeg-python
loguru GitHub: https://github.com/Delgan/loguru

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Callable ffmpeg-mcp Service
Output Introduction: Build and start SSE protocol ffmpeg-mcp service

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build funasr-mcp service, supporting speech recognition, converting audio files in wav and other formats to standard srt subtitle files

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements:
Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, capable of using basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-drive
Role Count: 1 person

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: funasr-mcp Service Build and Startup
Input Introduction: Install and start relevant MCP services by installing related Python dependencies
Input Example:
requirements.txt contains: loguru>=0.7.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, mcp>=1.0.0, torch, torchaudio, funasr, modelscope
Python code implements FastMCP service, using FunASR AutoModel (paraformer-zh, fsmn-vad, ct-punc-c, cam++) to provide audio2srt_text tool
Startup command: python server.py
Resource Links:
mcp-python-sdk GitHub: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk
funasr GitHub: https://github.com/modelscope/FunASR
loguru GitHub: https://github.com/Delgan/loguru

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Callable funasr-mcp Service
Output Introduction: Build and start SSE protocol funasr-mcp service

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build paddle-speech-tts-mcp service, supporting TTS, converting text to audio files

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements:
Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, capable of using basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-drive

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: paddle-speech-tts-mcp Service Build and Startup
Input Introduction: Install and start relevant MCP services by installing related Python dependencies
Input Example:
requirements.txt contains: loguru>=0.7.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, mcp>=1.0.0, paddlepaddle, pytest-runner, paddlespeech

steps.title: Video Compression Ratio Calculator MCP Service Build and Startup

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build custom MCP service for video compression ratio calculation (denoted as cus-tool-video_compression_calculator), used to calculate the compression duration allocation of multiple videos, ensuring that the total duration after compression does not exceed the target duration

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Backend/Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements: Familiar with Python
Role Count: 1

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Video Compression Ratio Calculator MCP Service Build and Startup
Input Introduction: Install and start relevant MCP services by installing related Python dependencies
Input Example:
requirements.txt contains: loguru>=0.7.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, mcp>=1.0.0

Python code implements FastMCP service, providing video_compression_calculator tool, core algorithm: try uniform compression ratio, if key video exceeds range, fix it at the boundary, strictly allocate remaining time to other videos to ensure sum = count_target
Startup command: python server.py
Resource Links:
mcp-python-sdk GitHub: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk
loguru GitHub: https://github.com/Delgan/loguru

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Callable Video Compression Ratio Calculator MCP Service
Output Introduction: Build and start SSE protocol video compression ratio calculator MCP service

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Configure and start song-generation service, supporting text-to-instrumental-music generation (denoted as bgm.wav)
Note: This step requires GPU and has certain configuration threshold, you can also use online products like https://suno.com/

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements:
Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, capable of using basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-drive
Role Count: 1 person

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: song-generation Service Configuration and Startup
Input Introduction: Install and start relevant web services by installing related Python dependencies
Input Example:
Pull docker image: docker pull juhayna/song-generation-levo:hf0613
Start container: docker run -it --gpus all --network=host juhayna/song-generation-levo:hf0613 /bin/bash
Download necessary resources and models (SongGeneration-base, SongGeneration-base-new, SongGeneration-base-full, SongGeneration-large)
Start web service: bash tools/gradio/run.sh your_ckpt_path
After startup, access http://IP:8081 in browser, enter text in the left text box, then click the button below to generate songs/instrumental music
Resource Links:
SongGeneration GitHub: https://github.com/tencent-ailab/SongGeneration

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Usable song-generation Web Service
Output Introduction: Configure and start gradio song-generation web service

subsections.title: Estimated Time
content: 1-2 days

steps.title: Generate Introduction Video Using Claude Code

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Claude Code to automatically call the previous MCP services to generate introduction video

subsections.title: Participants
content: Role Name: Algorithm Engineer
Skill Requirements:
Proficient in using various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (large models) after GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, capable of using basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-drive
Role Count: 1 person

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Generate Introduction Video Using Claude Code
Input Introduction: Interact with Claude Code to confirm input information to generate introduction video
Input Example:
First register the MCP services built and started in the previous steps in Claude Code:
claude mcp add -s user -t sse asr-funasr http://127.0.0.1:4005/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse tts-paddle_speech http://127.0.0.1:4006/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse cus-tool-video_compression_calculator http://127.0.0.1:4007/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse video-ffmpeg http://127.0.0.1:4008/sse
Pass in existing video materials and video_editing_skill.md
Video material requirements: files/ directory contains number.title.suffix (screen recording video files), text.txt (text/copy material files), bgm.wav (background music, optional)

video_editing_skill.md contains complete video production standardized process, including: environment preparation, input file validation, video content analysis and user confirmation, TTS audio generation and duration calculation, video/audio separation, duration matching compression, subtitle generation, text proofreading, video segment synthesis, video merging, background music adding and other stages
Start Claude Code in project root directory: claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
Enter prompt: Generate introduction video based on video materials and video_editing_skill.md
Resource Links:
Claude Code GitHub: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Introduction Video
Output Introduction: Final introduction video automatically edited by Claude Code
